title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men charged with kidnapping and assault of 28-year-old man on Essex Road near Winter Park.
Victim allegedly beaten, tied with extension cord, and taken to a residence; suffered abrasions and bleeding.
Suspects identified as Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett; arraignment scheduled for Monday.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested by ICE at Logan Airport while trying to board flights home.
Rosales was under a 2017 removal order despite pending DACA renewal; Ardila allegedly overstayed his visa.
Attorney Pomerleau reports increased ICE activity at Logan, with numerous recent immigration-related detentions.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Christopher Nolan's film 'The Odyssey' inspires a curated list of similar movies recommended by Kevin Slane.
Recommended films include 'Interstellar', 'Lawrence of Arabia', 'O Brother, Where Art Thou?', 'The Searchers', and 'The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie'.
'Obsession', directed by Curry Barker, becomes a 2026 horror hit grossing over $400 million on a $750,000 budget.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox achieve 11-game win streak, their longest since 2016, after sweeping Rays in a doubleheader.
Jake Bennett pitched six scoreless innings in Game 1, leading Red Sox to a 10-0 victory.
Red Sox improve to .500 on the season, securing the final AL wild-card spot with the Game 2 win.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early are progressing well in their recoveries and could return to the Red Sox soon.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo are making progress in rehabilitation and may return later this season.
Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, Isaiah Kiner-Falefa, and Marcelo Mayer remain sidelined with no set return date.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit three times including a home run and RBI, boosting his status in the Red Sox lineup.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7 with an RBI and scored two runs, pressuring Tampa Bay's pitching staff.
Carlos Narvaez excelled with 3 hits, a home run, and 3 RBIs, outperforming other catchers in the doubleheader.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Kraft supports Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel after offseason controversy involving reported affair allegations.
Mike Vrabel denied an affair with NFL reporter Dianna Russini, missing Day 3 of the NFL draft amid the news.
Dianna Russini resigned from The Athletic after an investigation into her conduct with Mike Vrabel.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Anya Taylor-Joy stars in 'Lucky,' a new crime series available on Apple TV, featuring a Las Vegas money heist storyline.
'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople,' with Sam Neill, is streaming on Prime Video and Plex, following a comedic adventure in New Zealand.
Horror film 'Obsession,' starring Michael Johnston, is a surprise hit on Peacock, grossing over $400 million from a $750,000 budget.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Concord man was found guilty of violating NH's Civil Rights Act after attacking a transgender woman in 2024.
The attacker, Travis Lufkin, was ordered to avoid the victim and her family and fined $5,000, with most suspended.
Lufkin also faced criminal charges, receiving a partly suspended 12-month sentence for second-degree assault.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox won their 10th consecutive game, positioning them as a formidable American League contender.
Chief baseball officer Craig Breslow considers adding talent before the Aug. 3 MLB trade deadline, with a focus on a right-handed hitter.
ESPN's Jeff Passan reports Boston's trade deadline plans are 'fluid', balancing recent success with past season mediocrity.
